support.summary: No CEO action required; $150k Pipeline evaluation a VP/RSD can carry.
support.detail: This is an early-stage ($150k, win 45, Formal Evaluation) white-label ANA partnership at EY UK with no identified Economic Buyer. Exec mapping to an EY Partner/MD is needed but is standard multi-threading the RSD/CRO can run via champion Stuart Ogilvie before his 31 July exit. Nothing here is blocked on a CEO-only lever (no discount ask, no roadmap commitment, no CEO-to-CEO peer relationship required at this size/stage).
support.metric: $150k amount, win 45, Pipeline (not large, not forecasted)
support.why_not_vp: Standard exec-mapping and demo scheduling on a $150k pipeline evaluation is squarely a CRO/RSD job; no CEO-irreplaceable lever is in play.

monitor.reason: On the 23 Jun call our own team judged the EY tender wired for a rival and debated walking away.

triggers.summary: EY tender looks wired for a rival; Zycus team openly debating whether to walk
triggers.detail: On the 23 Jun EY/Zycus re-group our team concluded the RFP 'does look like it's filtered in a certain way' and is 'set up for someone,' admitted 'we're not gonna hit those timelines' (needs ~9 months), and asked whether to 'completely walk from it.' Against SAP Ariba incumbency ('they are a big Ariba house') and an EY Build option, our differentiating Merlin ANA demo committed 19 May remains unscheduled 35 days later, with champion Stuart Ogilvie exiting 31 July and no handover secured. If ignored we forfeit a $150k white-label autonomous-negotiation foothold and a strategic EY SI channel to a pre-favored competitor.
triggers.metric: Merlin ANA demo 35 days overdue (committed 19 May, still unscheduled 23 Jun)
triggers.ceo_ask: Ask Claire whether the EY tender is wired for Ariba/CGI and if Zycus should invest or walk, and why the Merlin ANA demo is 35 days unscheduled with champion Stuart Ogilvie leaving 31 July.
triggers.evidence: 23 Jun call: 'it does look like it's filtered in a certain way ... I think it is set up for someone'; 'do we just ... completely walk from it?'; 'we're not gonna hit those timelines. It's gonna take us at least 9 months.'
